chief_complaint: A 46-year-old lady presented with a history of Cor Pulmonale and an incidental finding of antiphospholipid antibody syndrome.
history: The patient was diagnosed with antiphospholipid antibody syndrome (APS) and developed pulmonary hypertension and right heart failure. She had a history of prior pulmonary embolism. Antithrombotic therapy was the mainstay of treatment.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 90 bpm, RR 20 bpm, SpO2 92% on room air. Physical examination revealed signs of right heart failure.
labs: ECG: showing signs of right ventricular strain. Blood tests: positive for antiphospholipid antibodies. Imaging: echocardiogram showing pulmonary hypertension and right ventricular dysfunction.
correct_diagnosis: Antiphospholipid antibody syndrome with pulmonary hypertension and right heart failure
differentials: Antiphospholipid antibody syndrome with pulmonary hypertension and right heart failure
correct_ranking: Antiphospholipid antibody syndrome with pulmonary hypertension and right heart failure
